tasks.title: Add config and flag to toggle automatic git commit/push
evidence: User request: Add a configuration option and flag that allows the user to enable (default) or disable automatic git commit and git push; currently every successful loop finishes with an automatic git commit and git push; there is already a config option for git_revert_mode.
plan: Locate the current commit/push behavior in the run loop and how git_revert_mode is modeled in config/CLI.
Add a new config setting with default enabled plus a CLI flag to override it at runtime, wiring it into the commit/push decision path.
Update documentation and help output to describe the new setting/flag with examples.
Update prompts to reflect the new config and flag.
Add tests for config parsing, CLI overrides, and both enabled/disabled commit+push paths (including failure modes).
tasks.request: Add a configuration option and flag that allows the user to enable (default) or disable automatic git commit and git push. Currently every successful loop finishes with an automatic git commit and git push. We already have a config option for 'git_revert_mode' so we need a new config to toggle the automatic commit and push behavior.

tasks.title: Inject Phase 2 final response into Phase 3 prompt for context
evidence: User request: Pipe the FINAL response from the Phase 2 agent into the Phase 3 agent prompt, clearly labeled as context and not overriding Phase 3 guidelines.
plan: Locate where Phase 2 final responses are captured and where the Phase 3 prompt is assembled.
Append the captured Phase 2 response to the end of the Phase 3 prompt with explicit labeling that it is contextual and does not override Phase 3 instructions.
Update or add prompt tests to confirm the Phase 2 injection appears at the end and preserves Phase 3-specific guidance.
Verify prompt assembly output for Phase 3 includes the injection and passes existing validation checks.
tasks.request: Pipe the FINAL response from the Phase 2 agent into the Phase 3 agent. Capture the final Phase 2 response and add it to the end of the Phase 3 prompt. Clearly identify in the Phase 3 prompt that the Phase 2 agent injection is provided for context but it does not override the guidelines and instructions specific to the Phase 3 agent.

tasks.title: Align archive and status transition policy across CLI and TUI
evidence: Duplication audit: archive and status update logic duplicated in crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs and queue operations with differing timestamp handling and validation rules.
plan: Confirm the intended archive and status-transition rules for CLI and TUI (timestamps, validation, allowed transitions).
Introduce a shared mutation policy helper in queue operations that applies the agreed rules.
Update TUI and CLI paths to use the shared helper and remove duplicated logic.
Add tests covering terminal archive stamping and invalid/valid status transitions across both surfaces.
tasks.request: Read .ralph/cache/reports/duplication_and_consistency_audit.md and create tasks in the queue.json. Make sure to add all available fields, use depends_on, etc. Feel free to combine items in the report into single tasks to be executed together if necessary.

tasks.title: Unify runnable task selection for loop/run across CLI and TUI
evidence: Duplication audit: TUI next_loop_task_id re-implements next runnable selection and differs from queue::next_runnable_task and run_cmd::select_run_one_task_index (Doing priority and validation behavior).
plan: Define the intended runnable-task selection policy for loop and run-one (Doing priority, draft handling, validation errors).
Extract or extend a shared selection helper to encode the agreed policy.
Update TUI loop and CLI run selection to call the shared helper.
Add tests for selection ordering, draft handling, and target-id validation errors.
tasks.request: Read .ralph/cache/reports/duplication_and_consistency_audit.md and create tasks in the queue.json. Make sure to add all available fields, use depends_on, etc. Feel free to combine items in the report into single tasks to be executed together if necessary.

tasks.title: Align CLI and TUI search/filter pipeline with shared core
evidence: Duplication audit: CLI supports scope filtering and regex/case-sensitive search while TUI has fixed case-insensitive non-regex search and no scope filter.
plan: Define the shared search/filter semantics and which capabilities are surfaced in CLI vs TUI.
Introduce a shared filter/search core that accepts parameters for regex/case/scope behavior.
Update CLI and TUI to call the shared core with their respective options.
Add tests for regex/case/scope handling and TUI-specific defaults.
tasks.request: Read .ralph/cache/reports/duplication_and_consistency_audit.md and create tasks in the queue.json. Make sure to add all available fields, use depends_on, etc. Feel free to combine items in the report into single tasks to be executed together if necessary.

tasks.title: Consolidate formatting helpers and remove dead helper code
evidence: Duplication audit: formatting helpers duplicated across CLI/TUI; dead helpers exist in queue/operations/mutation.rs and redaction.rs; redundant custom-field formatting in tui/mod.rs.
plan: Identify shared semantic formatting behaviors that can be centralized without changing CLI/TUI presentation.
Consolidate duplicate formatting helpers and remove redundant custom-field formatting logic.
Remove dead helper code with allow(dead_code) where unused, updating tests accordingly.
Add tests for consolidated formatting and ensure removal does not regress existing outputs.

tasks.title: Add TUI flow to create a task from a description
evidence: User request: Add the functional equivalent of running 'ralph task \"Task description here\"' to the TUI.
plan: Locate the TUI command/action flow and identify the proper entry point for task creation.
Implement a TUI action that accepts a task description and invokes the same task creation path as `ralph task`.
Add tests covering successful task creation and validation/error cases for the new TUI action.
tasks.request: Add the functional equivalent of running 'ralph task \"Task description here\"' to the TUI

tasks.title: Add CLI parse tests for TUI entrypoint and interactive flags
evidence: Only a single interactive-related test exists (`cli_rejects_run_one_id_with_interactive`) in `crates/ralph/src/cli/mod.rs`; there are no tests ensuring valid `-i` paths parse correctly.
plan: Add CLI parsing tests for `ralph run one -i` and `ralph run loop -i`.
If a new TUI subcommand is added, add parse tests for it.
Ensure tests cover negative and positive interactive cases.
